chat_messages.text: I'm trying to find a quote. It goes, something like "any great discovery is attacked at first, then included in privileged discourse only, then it is simply another fact"

content.text: I'm trying to find a quote. It goes, something like "any great discovery is attacked at first, then included in privileged discourse only, then it is simply another fact"

chat_messages.text:
This sounds similar to a quote often attributed to Arthur Schopenhauer, which goes: "All truth passes through three stages. First, it is ridiculed. Second, it is violently opposed. Third, it is accepted as being self-evident."

There's also a similar quote attributed to John Stuart Mill: "Every great movement must experience three stages: ridicule, discussion, adoption."

However, I should note that while these attributions are common, I can't verify with certainty that either philosopher actually wrote or said these exact words. The concept they express - about how new ideas progress from rejection to acceptance - has been articulated in various ways by different thinkers throughout history.

Would either of these be the quote you were thinking of? Or perhaps you're remembering a different version?
